debug_info.goal:
C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)
hC : Convex ℝ C
x y : Fin 2 → Fin 3 → ℝ
z : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) → Fin 3 → ℝ
hxy : ∀ (i j : Fin 2) (s : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2)), x i + y j + z s ∈ C ↔ (i, j) ∈ s
⊢ False

failed_attempts: sorry

by
  have h1 : (0, 0) ∉ ({(0, 1), (1, 0)} : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2)) := by decide
  have h2 : x 0 + y 0 + z {(0, 1), (1, 0)} ∉ C := by rw [hxy]; exact h1
  have h3 : x 0 + y 1 + z {(0, 1), (1, 0)} ∈ C := by rw [hxy]; simp
  have h4 : x 1 + y 0 + z {(0, 1), (1, 0)} ∈ C := by rw [hxy]; simp
  have h5 : x 1 + y 1 + z {(0, 1), (1, 0)} ∉ C := by rw [hxy]; decide
  have h6 : x 0 + y 0 + z {(0, 0), (1, 1)} ∈ C := by rw [hxy]; simp
  have h7 : x 1 + y 1 + z {(0, 0), (1, 1)} ∈ C := by rw [hxy]; simp
  sorry
sorry
sorry

by
  let s₁ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 0), (1, 1)}
  let s₂ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 1), (1, 0)}
  have h00₁ : x 0 + y 0 + z s₁ ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 s₁).mpr (by simp [s₁])
  have h11₁ : x 1 + y 1 + z s₁ ∈ C := (hxy 1 1 s₁).mpr (by simp [s₁])
  have h01₁ : x 0 + y 1 + z s₁ ∉ C := (hxy 0 1 s₁).not.mpr (by simp [s₁])
  have h10₁ : x 1 + y 0 + z s₁ ∉ C := (hxy 1 0 s₁).not.mpr (by simp [s₁])
  have h01₂ : x 0 + y 1 + z s₂ ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 s₂).mpr (by simp [s₂])
  have h10₂ : x 1 + y 0 + z s₂ ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 s₂).mpr (by simp [s₂])
  have h00₂ : x 0 + y 0 + z s₂ ∉ C := (hxy 0 0 s₂).not.mpr (by simp [s₂])
  have h11₂ : x 1 + y 1 + z s₂ ∉ C := (hxy 1 1 s₂).not.mpr (by simp [s₂])
  have hmid : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s₁) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s₁) =
              (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s₁) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s₁) := by ring
  have hconv : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s₁) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s₁) ∈ C :=
    hC h00₁ h11₁ (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  rw [hmid] at hconv
  have hmid' : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s₂) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s₂) =
               (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s₂) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s₂) := by ring
  have hconv' : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s₂) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s₂) ∈ C :=
    hC h01₂ h10₂ (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  sorry

by
  have := vc_lt_four_of_convex_r2 hC (x := fun k => x (k / 2) + y (k % 2)) 
    (y := fun s => z (Set.image (fun k => (k / 2, k % 2)) s))
  exact this fun i s => by simp only [hxy]; constructor <;> intro h <;> simpa using h

by
  -- The four points x i + y j form a parallelogram: (x 0 + y 0) + (x 1 + y 1) = (x 0 + y 1) + (x 1 + y 0)
  -- Consider s = {(0,0), (1,1)} and s' = {(0,1), (1,0)}
  let s₁ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 0), (1, 1)}
  let s₂ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 1), (1, 0)}
  have h00₁ : x 0 + y 0 + z s₁ ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 s₁).mpr (by simp [s₁])
  have h11₁ : x 1 + y 1 + z s₁ ∈ C := (hxy 1 1 s₁).mpr (by simp [s₁])
  have h01₁ : x 0 + y 1 + z s₁ ∉ C := (hxy 0 1 s₁).not.mpr (by simp [s₁])
  have h10₁ : x 1 + y 0 + z s₁ ∉ C := (hxy 1 0 s₁).not.mpr (by simp [s₁])
  -- By convexity, the midpoint of (x 0 + y 0 + z s₁) and (x 1 + y 1 + z s₁) is in C
  have hmid : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s₁) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s₁) ∈ C := by
    apply hC h00₁ h11₁ (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  -- This midpoint equals (1/2)(x 0 + y 1 + z s₁) + (1/2)(x 1 + y 0 + z s₁)
  have heq : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s₁) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s₁) = 
             (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s₁) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s₁) := by
    simp only [smul_add, ← add_assoc]
    ring_nf
    ext i
    simp only [Pi.add_apply, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]
    ring
  -- So this point is also the midpoint of two points NOT in C
  -- For s₂, we need (0,1) and (1,0) in C, (0,0) and (1,1) not in C
  have h01₂ : x 0 + y 1 + z s₂ ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 s₂).mpr (by simp [s₂])
  have h10₂ : x 1 + y 0 + z s₂ ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 s₂).mpr (by simp [s₂])
  have h00₂ : x 0 + y 0 + z s₂ ∉ C := (hxy 0 0 s₂).not.mpr (by simp [s₂])
  have h11₂ : x 1 + y 1 + z s₂ ∉ C := (hxy 1 1 s₂).not.mpr (by simp [s₂])
  have hmid₂ : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s₂) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s₂) ∈ C := by
    apply hC h01₂ h10₂ (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  sorry

by
  -- Consider the sets {(0,0), (1,1)} and {(0,1), (1,0)}
  let s₁ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 0), (1, 1)}
  let s₂ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 1), (1, 0)}
  -- Points in C from s₁: x 0 + y 0 + z s₁ and x 1 + y 1 + z s₁
  have h00_s1 : x 0 + y 0 + z s₁ ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 s₁).mpr (Or.inl rfl)
  have h11_s1 : x 1 + y 1 + z s₁ ∈ C := (hxy 1 1 s₁).mpr (Or.inr rfl)
  -- Points in C from s₂: x 0 + y 1 + z s₂ and x 1 + y 0 + z s₂
  have h01_s2 : x 0 + y 1 + z s₂ ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 s₂).mpr (Or.inl rfl)
  have h10_s2 : x 1 + y 0 + z s₂ ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 s₂).mpr (Or.inr rfl)
  -- By convexity, midpoint of (x 0 + y 0 + z s₁) and (x 1 + y 1 + z s₁) is in C
  have mid1 : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s₁) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s₁) ∈ C :=
    hC h00_s1 h11_s1 (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  -- By convexity, midpoint of (x 0 + y 1 + z s₂) and (x 1 + y 0 + z s₂) is in C
  have mid2 : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s₂) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s₂) ∈ C :=
    hC h01_s2 h10_s2 (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  -- Both midpoints equal (1/2)(x 0 + x 1 + y 0 + y 1) + (1/2)(z s₁ + z s₁) for mid1
  -- and (1/2)(x 0 + x 1 + y 0 + y 1) + (1/2)(z s₂ + z s₂) for mid2
  -- The x+y parts are the same: (1/2)(x 0 + x 1 + y 0 + y 1)
  -- Consider empty set: no point should be in C
  have h00_empty : x 0 + y 0 + z ∅ ∉ C := by simp [hxy]
  have h01_empty : x 0 + y 1 + z ∅ ∉ C := by simp [hxy]
  have h10_empty : x 1 + y 0 + z ∅ ∉ C := by simp [hxy]
  have h11_empty : x 1 + y 1 + z ∅ ∉ C := by simp [hxy]
  -- Consider full set: all points should be in C
  let full : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := Set.univ
  have h00_full : x 0 + y 0 + z full ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 full).mpr (Set.mem_univ _)
  have h01_full : x 0 + y 1 + z full ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 full).mpr (Set.mem_univ _)
  have h10_full : x 1 + y 0 + z full ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 full).mpr (Set.mem_univ _)
  have h11_full : x 1 + y 1 + z full ∈ C := (hxy 1 1 full).mpr (Set.mem_univ _)
  -- Midpoint of all four full points
  have mid_full : (1/4 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z full) + (1/4 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z full) +
                  (1/4 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z full) + (1/4 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z full) ∈ C := by
    have h1 : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z full) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z full) ∈ C :=
      hC h00_full h11_full (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
    have h2 : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z full) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z full) ∈ C :=
      hC h01_full h10_full (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
    have := hC h1 h2 (by norm_num : (0:ℝ) ≤ 1/2) (by norm_num : (0:ℝ) ≤ 1/2) (by norm_num : (1/2:ℝ) + 1/2 = 1)
    convert this using 1
    simp only [smul_add]
    ring
  sorry
sorry
sorry

by
  -- Consider the diagonal set {(0,0), (1,1)} and anti-diagonal {(0,1), (1,0)}
  let s₁ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 0), (1, 1)}
  let s₂ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 1), (1, 0)}
  -- Points x 0 + y 0 + z s₁ and x 1 + y 1 + z s₁ are in C
  have h00 : x 0 + y 0 + z s₁ ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 s₁).mpr (Or.inl rfl)
  have h11 : x 1 + y 1 + z s₁ ∈ C := (hxy 1 1 s₁).mpr (Or.inr rfl)
  -- Points x 0 + y 1 + z s₂ and x 1 + y 0 + z s₂ are in C
  have h01 : x 0 + y 1 + z s₂ ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 s₂).mpr (Or.inl rfl)
  have h10 : x 1 + y 0 + z s₂ ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 s₂).mpr (Or.inr rfl)
  -- By convexity, midpoint of (0,0) and (1,1) with z s₁ is in C
  -- and midpoint of (0,1) and (1,0) with z s₂ is in C
  -- The empty set should have no points
  let s₀ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := ∅
  have hne00 : ¬(0, 0) ∈ s₀ := Set.not_mem_empty _
  have hne01 : ¬(0, 1) ∈ s₀ := Set.not_mem_empty _
  have hne10 : ¬(1, 0) ∈ s₀ := Set.not_mem_empty _
  have hne11 : ¬(1, 1) ∈ s₀ := Set.not_mem_empty _
  -- The full set should have all points
  let s_full : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := Set.univ
  have hf00 : x 0 + y 0 + z s_full ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 s_full).mpr (Set.mem_univ _)
  have hf11 : x 1 + y 1 + z s_full ∈ C := (hxy 1 1 s_full).mpr (Set.mem_univ _)
  have hf01 : x 0 + y 1 + z s_full ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 s_full).mpr (Set.mem_univ _)
  have hf10 : x 1 + y 0 + z s_full ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 s_full).mpr (Set.mem_univ _)
  -- Midpoint of diagonal in full set
  have hmid : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s_full) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s_full) ∈ C :=
    hC hf00 hf11 (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  have hmid' : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s_full) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s_full) ∈ C :=
    hC hf01 hf10 (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  -- These midpoints are equal: (x0+x1)/2 + (y0+y1)/2 + z s_full
  -- This should equal some x i + y j + z s for some s, but z varies with s
  -- The real contradiction: consider s = {(0,0)} only
  let s₃ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 0)}
  have hin : x 0 + y 0 + z s₃ ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 s₃).mpr rfl
  have hout01 : x 0 + y 1 + z s₃ ∉ C := (hxy 0 1 s₃).not.mpr (by simp [s₃])
  have hout10 : x 1 + y 0 + z s₃ ∉ C := (hxy 1 0 s₃).not.mpr (by simp [s₃])
  have hout11 : x 1 + y 1 + z s₃ ∉ C := (hxy 1 1 s₃).not.mpr (by simp [s₃])
  sorry

by
  have h1 : x 0 + y 0 + z {(0, 0), (1, 1)} ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 _).mpr (by simp)
  have h2 : x 1 + y 1 + z {(0, 0), (1, 1)} ∈ C := (hxy 1 1 _).mpr (by simp)
  have h3 : x 0 + y 1 + z {(0, 0), (1, 1)} ∉ C := by
    simp only [hxy]
    decide
  have h4 : x 1 + y 0 + z {(0, 0), (1, 1)} ∉ C := by
    simp only [hxy]
    decide
  have heq : x 0 + y 0 + x 1 + y 1 = x 0 + y 1 + x 1 + y 0 := by ring
  have hmid : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z {(0, 0), (1, 1)}) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z {(0, 0), (1, 1)}) = 
              (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z {(0, 0), (1, 1)}) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z {(0, 0), (1, 1)}) := by
    simp only [smul_add]
    module
  have hconv := hC.add_smul_mem h1 h2 (by norm_num : (0 : ℝ) ≤ 1) (by norm_num : 1 ≤ (1 : ℝ))
  have h5 : x 0 + y 1 + z {(0, 1), (1, 0)} ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 _).mpr (by simp)
  have h6 : x 1 + y 0 + z {(0, 1), (1, 0)} ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 _).mpr (by simp)
  have h7 : x 0 + y 0 + z {(0, 1), (1, 0)} ∉ C := by simp only [hxy]; decide
  have h8 : x 1 + y 1 + z {(0, 1), (1, 0)} ∉ C := by simp only [hxy]; decide
  have hconv2 := hC h5 h6 (by norm_num : (0 : ℝ) ≤ 1/2) (by norm_num : (0 : ℝ) ≤ 1/2) (by norm_num : (1/2 : ℝ) + 1/2 = 1)
  have hconv1 := hC h1 h2 (by norm_num : (0 : ℝ) ≤ 1/2) (by norm_num : (0 : ℝ) ≤ 1/2) (by norm_num : (1/2 : ℝ) + 1/2 = 1)
  have key : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1) = (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0) := by
    simp only [smul_add]; module
  sorry

by
  -- Consider the set s = {(0,0), (0,1), (1,0)} which excludes (1,1)
  let s : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 0), (0, 1), (1, 0)}
  -- The points x 0 + y 0 + z s, x 0 + y 1 + z s, x 1 + y 0 + z s are in C
  have h00 : x 0 + y 0 + z s ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 s).mpr (by simp [s])
  have h01 : x 0 + y 1 + z s ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 s).mpr (by simp [s])
  have h10 : x 1 + y 0 + z s ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 s).mpr (by simp [s])
  have h11 : (1, 1) ∉ s := by simp [s]; omega
  have h11' : x 1 + y 1 + z s ∉ C := (hxy 1 1 s).not.mpr h11
  -- Key: x 0 + y 0 + x 1 + y 1 = x 0 + y 1 + x 1 + y 0
  -- So (x 1 + y 1 + z s) = (x 0 + y 0 + z s) + (x 1 + y 1 + z s) - ... is a convex combination
  -- Actually: midpoint of (x 0 + y 0 + z s) and (x 1 + y 1 + z s) equals midpoint of (x 0 + y 1 + z s) and (x 1 + y 0 + z s)
  have hmid : x 0 + y 0 + z s + (x 1 + y 1 + z s) = x 0 + y 1 + z s + (x 1 + y 0 + z s) := by
    ext i; simp only [Pi.add_apply]; ring
  -- The midpoint of h01 and h10 is in C
  have hconv : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s) ∈ C :=
    hC h01 h10 (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  -- This equals (1/2) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s) + (1/2) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s)
  have heq : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s) = 
             (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s) := by
    ext i; simp only [Pi.add_apply, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]; ring
  -- So the midpoint of h00 and the point x 1 + y 1 + z s is in C
  rw [heq] at hconv
  -- By convexity, since h00 ∈ C and midpoint ∈ C, we can show x 1 + y 1 + z s ∈ C
  have : x 1 + y 1 + z s = 2 • ((1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s)) - (x 0 + y 0 + z s) := by
    ext i; simp only [Pi.add_apply, Pi.smul_apply, Pi.sub_apply, smul_eq_mul]; ring
  -- We need a different approach - use that x 1 + y 1 + z s = 2 * midpoint - (x 0 + y 0 + z s)
  -- midpoint = (1/2)(x 0 + y 0 + z s) + (1/2)(x 1 + y 1 + z s)
  -- So x 1 + y 1 + z s = 2 * midpoint - (x 0 + y 0 + z s)
  have key : x 1 + y 1 + z s ∈ C := by
    have := hC h00 hconv (by norm_num : (0:ℝ) ≤ -1 + 2) (by norm_num : (0:ℝ) ≤ 2) (by ring)
    convert this using 1
    ext i; simp only [Pi.add_apply, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]; ring
  exact h11' key

by
  -- Consider the set s = {(0,0), (1,1)} (diagonal)
  let s : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 0), (1, 1)}
  -- x 0 + y 0 + z s ∈ C and x 1 + y 1 + z s ∈ C
  have h00 : x 0 + y 0 + z s ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 s).mpr (by simp [s])
  have h11 : x 1 + y 1 + z s ∈ C := (hxy 1 1 s).mpr (by simp [s])
  -- By convexity, midpoint is in C
  have hmid : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s) ∈ C :=
    hC h00 h11 (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  -- This midpoint equals (1/2)(x 0 + y 1 + z s) + (1/2)(x 1 + y 0 + z s)
  have heq : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s) =
             (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s) := by
    simp only [smul_add]
    module
  -- But (0,1) ∉ s and (1,0) ∉ s
  have h01 : x 0 + y 1 + z s ∉ C := by simp only [hxy, s]; decide
  have h10 : x 1 + y 0 + z s ∉ C := by simp only [hxy, s]; decide
  -- Now consider s' = {(0,1), (1,0)} and get a contradiction
  let s' : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 1), (1, 0)}
  have h01' : x 0 + y 1 + z s' ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 s').mpr (by simp [s'])
  have h10' : x 1 + y 0 + z s' ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 s').mpr (by simp [s'])
  have h00' : x 0 + y 0 + z s' ∉ C := by simp only [hxy, s']; decide
  have h11' : x 1 + y 1 + z s' ∉ C := by simp only [hxy, s']; decide
  have hmid' : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s') + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s') ∈ C :=
    hC h01' h10' (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)
  have heq' : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s') + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s') =
              (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s') + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s') := by
    simp only [smul_add]
    module
  sorry

by
  -- Consider the sets s₁ = {(0,0), (1,1)} and s₂ = {(0,1), (1,0)}
  let s₁ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 0), (1, 1)}
  let s₂ : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 1), (1, 0)}
  -- From hxy, the points x 0 + y 0 + z s₁ and x 1 + y 1 + z s₁ are in C
  have h00_s1 : x 0 + y 0 + z s₁ ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 s₁).mpr (Or.inl rfl)
  have h11_s1 : x 1 + y 1 + z s₁ ∈ C := (hxy 1 1 s₁).mpr (Or.inr rfl)
  -- But x 0 + y 1 + z s₁ and x 1 + y 0 + z s₁ are not in C
  have h01_s1 : x 0 + y 1 + z s₁ ∉ C := by simp only [hxy, s₁, Set.mem_insert_iff, Set.mem_singleton_iff, Prod.mk.injEq]; decide
  have h10_s1 : x 1 + y 0 + z s₁ ∉ C := by simp only [hxy, s₁, Set.mem_insert_iff, Set.mem_singleton_iff, Prod.mk.injEq]; decide
  -- Similarly for s₂
  have h01_s2 : x 0 + y 1 + z s₂ ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 s₂).mpr (Or.inl rfl)
  have h10_s2 : x 1 + y 0 + z s₂ ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 s₂).mpr (Or.inr rfl)
  have h00_s2 : x 0 + y 0 + z s₂ ∉ C := by simp only [hxy, s₂, Set.mem_insert_iff, Set.mem_singleton_iff, Prod.mk.injEq]; decide
  have h11_s2 : x 1 + y 1 + z s₂ ∉ C := by simp only [hxy, s₂, Set.mem_insert_iff, Set.mem_singleton_iff, Prod.mk.injEq]; decide

  -- Points (0,1) and (1,0) are in C for the second diagonal set
  -- By convexity, midpoints should satisfy certain properties

  let s2 : Set (Fin 2 × Fin 2) := {(0, 1), (1, 0)}
  have h00_s1 : x 0 + y 0 + z s1 ∈ C := (hxy 0 0 s1).mpr (by simp [s1])
  have h11_s1 : x 1 + y 1 + z s1 ∈ C := (hxy 1 1 s1).mpr (by simp [s1])
  have h01_s1 : x 0 + y 1 + z s1 ∉ C := by simp only [hxy, s1]; decide
  have h01_s2 : x 0 + y 1 + z s2 ∈ C := (hxy 0 1 s2).mpr (by simp [s2])
  have h10_s2 : x 1 + y 0 + z s2 ∈ C := (hxy 1 0 s2).mpr (by simp [s2])
  have h00_s2 : x 0 + y 0 + z s2 ∉ C := by simp only [hxy, s2]; decide
  -- The midpoint of (0,0)+z(s1) and (1,1)+z(s1) equals midpoint of (0,1)+z(s2) and (1,0)+z(s2)
  -- shifted by (z(s1) - z(s2))/2, leading to contradiction
  have hmid : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s1) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s1) =
              (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s1) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s1) := by
    simp only [smul_add, ← add_assoc]
    ring_nf
    congr 1 <;> ring
  have hconv1 : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 0 + z s1) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 1 + z s1) ∈ C :=
    hC h00_s1 h11_s1 (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by norm_num)

  have hconv2 : (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 0 + y 1 + z s1) + (1/2 : ℝ) • (x 1 + y 0 + z s1) ∈ C := hconv1
  sorry
